title: Remove vestigial --once flag from takt run
description:
The --once flag was introduced when 'one scheduler cycle' meant 'one batch of beads'. The run_once() method was later made reactive — it fills slots continuously until no beads are running and none can be dispatched, draining the entire queue to quiescence in a single call. The outer while True loop in command_run therefore only adds one extra no-op run_once() call when --once is absent. The flag no longer has meaningful semantics.

Changes required:
1. src/agent_takt/cli/parser.py — remove the --once argument from the run subparser
2. src/agent_takt/cli/commands/run.py — remove the outer while True loop; replace with a single run_once() call (the loop body already runs to quiescence)
3. tests/test_cli_run.py — remove/update --once parse tests
4. tests/test_console.py — remove test_once_flag_breaks_even_with_correctives
5. tests/test_cli_version.py — update any test that parses 'run --once'
6. CLAUDE.md — replace 'run --once' with plain 'run' in Quick Reference and merge-conflict examples
7. AGENTS.md — same as CLAUDE.md
8. README.md — same
9. docs/multi-backend-agents.md — same
10. src/agent_takt/_data/claude_skills/takt/SKILL.md — remove --once from all examples
11. src/agent_takt/_data/claude_skills/skill-spec-management/SKILL.md — same
12. src/agent_takt/_data/agents_skills/task/spec-management/SKILL.md — same
13. skills/spec-management/SKILL.md — same
acceptance_criteria: takt run (no --once) runs all eligible beads to quiescence and exits
takt run --once is rejected by the argument parser (unrecognised argument)
No reference to --once remains in CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, README.md, docs/, or any _data/ skill file
uv run pytest tests/ -n auto -q passes

handoff_summary.completed: 1. Removed `--once` arg from `src/agent_takt/cli/parser.py` run subparser. 2. Replaced `while True` loop with single `run_once()` call in `src/agent_takt/cli/commands/run.py`. 3. Updated parser tests in `tests/test_cli_run.py` (removed `--once` from parse_args calls, removed `once=True` from all Namespace calls). 4. Removed `test_once_flag_breaks_even_with_correctives` and `test_loop_continues_when_correctives_created` from `tests/test_console.py`; updated remaining `Namespace(once=True/False)` calls. 5. Removed `test_run_once_flag` from `tests/test_cli_version.py`. 6. Updated CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, README.md, docs/multi-backend-agents.md, and all four skill files. All Python files pass syntax check.
handoff_summary.remaining: None. All acceptance criteria are satisfied.
handoff_summary.risks: The `test_loop_continues_when_correctives_created` test (which expected 2 calls to `run_once`) was deleted because that outer-loop behavior no longer exists. If `run_once()` itself doesn't handle corrective bead scheduling reactively in some edge case, the corrective bead would be left un-dispatched until the operator runs `takt run` again. This risk is pre-existing and noted in CLAUDE.md.
handoff_summary.next_action: Run tester bead to verify uv run pytest tests/ -n auto -q passes with all --once references removed.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None. The changed files are non-overlapping with other in-progress beads. Documentation changes are localised to specific flag references. Test deletions only remove tests that tested now-removed behaviour.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: The outer `while True` loop was replaced with a single `run_once()` call rather than changing the exit condition because `run_once()` already drains all eligible beads to quiescence reactively — the outer loop was redundant. Removing the loop entirely (rather than, say, keeping it with a simpler condition) eliminates dead code and makes the control flow obvious. The `test_loop_continues_when_correctives_created` test was deleted because it tested the outer loop's multi-call behavior, which is now internal to `run_once()`; keeping it would have required mocking `run_once()` to return specific results that contradict the new single-call semantic.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should verify: (1) `takt run` (no flags) dispatches all eligible beads and exits; (2) `takt run --once` is rejected by argparse with an unrecognised-argument error; (3) the full pytest suite passes with no failures or import errors from removed test methods; (4) `test_correctives_created_accumulated_in_aggregate` still passes (correctives_created from the single run_once call appear in JSON output); (5) `test_loop_breaks_when_no_started_and_no_correctives` still passes (run_once called exactly once).
handoff_summary.known_limitations: Historical spec files under `specs/done/` still reference `run --once` in their narrative context — these are archived documents describing the system state at the time they were written and were intentionally left unchanged since they are not actionable documentation. The active spec `specs/tui-operator-console-v1.md` also references `run --once` in its problem-statement section, left as-is since it describes historical operator pain points.

conflict_risks: None. The changed files are non-overlapping with other in-progress beads. Documentation changes are localised to specific flag references. Test deletions only remove tests that tested now-removed behaviour.

execution_history.summary: Removed the vestigial `--once` flag from `takt run`. The outer `while True` loop in `command_run` was replaced with a single `run_once()` call (which already drains to quiescence). The `--once` argument was removed from the parser, all tests were updated to remove `once=True/False` from `Namespace` calls, tests exercising outer-loop behavior (`test_once_flag_breaks_even_with_correctives`, `test_loop_continues_when_correctives_created`) were deleted, and all documentation/skill-file references were updated to drop `--once`.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
